commit.message:
chore: drop Railway volume persistence; add on-demand decision log export (#37)

* chore: drop Railway volume persistence; add on-demand decision log export

Replace file-system audit log persistence (was writing to a Railway
volume, 362 MB) with in-memory-only storage and a new on-demand export
mechanism.  Logs reset on redeploy — accepted trade-off for eliminating
the volume cost.

Changes:
- decision_center/app.py: remove DECISION_CENTER_PERSISTENCE_PATH
  wiring; add GET /v1/logs/export (returns full audit store as a JSON
  attachment)
- decision_center/cli.py: add download_decision_log helper and a new
  interactive mode 3 "Download Decision Log"
- decision_center/tests/test_api.py: add
  test_export_logs_returns_full_store_as_attachment
- ui/src/api.ts: add downloadDecisionLog() helper
- ui/src/components/DecisionLog.tsx: add "Download JSON" button wired
  to the new export endpoint
- docs/deployment-railway.md, README.md, CLAUDE.md: update deployment
  notes to reflect in-memory model and on-demand export

Tests: 306 passed (5 pre-existing failures in evals unrelated to this change).
UI lint and build clean.

* feat(workflow-sdk): add parallel session execution with OpenTUI graph panel

Introduce parallel step execution in the workflow engine and rewrite the
orchestrator panel as an OpenTUI React component tree.

Workflow API changes:
- .session() now accepts a single SessionOptions (sequential) or an
  array of SessionOptions (parallel within one step)
- WorkflowDefinition.sessions → .steps (array of step groups)
- Duplicate-name validation across all steps

Executor changes:
- Extract runSingleSession() helper for reuse across sequential and
  parallel paths
- Add promiseAllFailFast() with AbortController for fail-fast parallel
  execution — cancels siblings on first failure
- Parallel sessions block transcript/getMessages access to siblings
  (only prior-step data is available)

Panel rewrite:
- Replace imperative OrchestratorPanel (panel.ts) with OpenTUI React
  components under src/components/
- Session graph layout with fan-out/fan-in edges, node cards, status
  indicators, and a header/statusline
- Catppuccin Mocha/Latte theme via OpenTUI themeMode detection, replacing
  OSC terminal palette queries

Supporting:
- Add killWindow() tmux helper for cleaning up parallel panes on failure
- Export killWindow from SDK index
- Enable JSX with @opentui/react in tsconfig
- Update discovery test assertions for steps shape

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Each follows the same pattern:

  2. Parallel: two agents summarize concurrently (bullet points + narrative)
  3. Sequential: merge both summaries into a final output

These demonstrate the new .session([...]) parallel step API.

* fix(workflow-sdk): pipe stderr in attachSession to prevent terminal escape leakage

Tmux writes terminal capability queries to inherited stderr, which
pollutes test output with raw escape sequences. Pipe stderr instead
and include it in the thrown error message for better diagnostics.

Assistant-model: Claude Code

* test(workflow-sdk): remove create() test that leaks terminal escape sequences

OrchestratorPanel.create() instantiates a real CLI renderer which
queries terminal capabilities — the xterm.js responses pollute test
output. The factory is a thin wrapper over createCliRenderer and
createWithRenderer, both already tested independently.

Assistant-model: Claude Code

* fix(workflow-sdk): address PR review feedback across executor, layout, store, and discovery

- Harden shell escaping (escBash/escPwsh): strip null bytes, export for
  testability, add 30 edge-case tests covering unicode, combined specials,
  and control characters
- Add 100ms timestamp buffer for Claude session detection to prevent
  race conditions in fast sequential runs
- Consolidate repeated abort-signal checks into throwIfAborted() helper
- Add collision detection to layout algorithm: resolve horizontal node
  overlaps after merge-node placement, with invariant tests across all
  topologies
- Return early (no emit) in PanelStore when session name is unknown,
  preventing useless re-renders
- Replace hardcoded node_modules skip in discovery with gitignore-based
  filtering via `ignore` package — workflows .gitignore is auto-generated
  and regenerated if missing
- Export WORKFLOWS_GITIGNORE constant and use it in install-workflows.ts
  as single source of truth
- Document .claude/skills symlink and Windows Developer Mode requirement
  in .gitattributes

* fix(workflow-sdk): fix empty-array hang and session race in executor

Add early return in promiseAllFailFast for empty arrays, which
previously caused a hang since resolve() was only called inside

Replace timestamp-only Claude session filtering with a pre-run
snapshot of existing session IDs, so concurrent workflows sharing
the same working directory cannot pick up each other's sessions.

commit.message:
Fix AOF persistence and WATCH for collection-emptying RMW ops (#1678)

Bug 1: Collection-emptying RMW operations (LPOP, ZREM, HDEL, SREM that
empty a collection) were not logged to AOF. InPlaceUpdaterWorker and
PostCopyUpdater returned false before setting NeedAofLog when
HasRemoveKey was true, so PostRMWOperation never wrote the AOF entry.
Data reappeared after restart.

Bug 2: InPlaceUpdaterWorker's HasRemoveKey path did not call
IncrementVersion on the watch version map, so WATCH/MULTI/EXEC
transactions did not detect the key modification and incorrectly
committed.

Bug 3: AofProcessor.RecoverReplay disposed respServerSession in its
finally block, but in multi-DB recovery it ran once per database,
causing double-dispose and NullReferenceException in
GarnetLatencyMetricsSession.Return(). Moved dispose to
AofProcessor.Dispose() which runs exactly once.

Fixes microsoft/garnet#1675

commit.message:
r600: resolve 3 hostile-review gaps — KCACHE proof, VTX tail-byte, trash page

Address 3 architectural gaps raised by hostile review of Phase 6 probe results.

Gap 10 (KCACHE Poison Leak): Strengthen KCACHE Tier 1 eligibility to require
entire load-footprint proof (not just base index). minUniformBufferOffsetAlignment
already 256 (confirmed by vulkaninfo). Compiler must prove
(index*stride + load_size) <= range for vec4/mat4 loads and spec-constant
indices. Static OOB loads folded to zero at compile time.

Gap 11 (VTX Dword-Clamp Truncation): Advertise robustStorageBufferAccessSizeAlignment=4
making ALIGN_POT(range,4) spec-compliant. Targeted tail-byte ALU mask only
activates for robust reads touching the final partial dword — not a global tax.

Gap 12 (Math Predication Offset-Zero Corruption): DEPRECATE offset×0 pattern
which corrupts valid data at buffer offset 0. Replace with trash-page redirect:
4KB VRAM page allocated at vkCreateDevice, fixed address in robustness metadata
(KCACHE bank 14, byte 48). OOB writes select final address (not base+offset)
preventing offset escape. Returning atomics still use PRED_SEL zeroing.

Updated: ABI contract §5 (Tier 2 revised), §10c (3 new gaps), §11 Q8
(offset×0 deprecated), §12.6-12.7 (compiler requirements). Blueprint
terakan_shader_args.h §2 (52-byte metadata with trash_page_addr) and §3
(Tier 2 trash-page in struct comments). OOB findings and README updated.

Rubber-duck validated: 9th independent review confirmed solutions. Key
critique adopted: select final address not base (prevents offset escape),
alignment alone insufficient (compiler proof is real fix), tail-byte mask
is targeted slow-path only (not global ALU tax).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
EXP-1481-1490: Clinical translation & ADA guideline validation

10 experiments translating pipeline to clinical practice:
- EXP-1481: 9/11 insulin-sensitive phenotype (AID amplifies)
- EXP-1482: Dose-response R²<0.08 — AID confounds ISF calibration
- EXP-1483: ISF circadian amplitude 12%, sinusoidal fit poor
- EXP-1484: Personalized targets inflate TIR, keep standard 70-180
- EXP-1485: 8/11 overcount carbs (systematic bias)
- EXP-1486: AID target range 64-149 mg/dL (huge config variation)
- EXP-1487: Natural experiments best for CR, worst for ISF
- EXP-1488: Long-term projections unreliable beyond 3 months
- EXP-1489: Clinical reports average 117 words (workflow-ready)
- EXP-1490: Pipeline-ADA alignment only 64% — TBR not tracked

CRITICAL: Pipeline needs TBR (time-below-range) safety check.
5/11 patients exceed ADA 4% TBR threshold undetected.
Pipeline v10 proposal: add TBR to grading formula (15% weight).

test(emitter): cover False branch of config_candidates check in emit_extension_files

When an extension artifact has raw_content set, emit_extension_files uses
find_map to locate which file in artifact.files is the config source.  The
find_map closure evaluates each file name against config_candidates.  All
existing tests ordered the config file first in the files vec, so the closure
always returned Some immediately and the False branch (non-config filename)
was never reached.

Add emit_extension_files_non_config_before_config_covers_false_branch which
places index.js before config.json in artifact.files.  The find_map now
evaluates index.js first (False — not a config candidate) before finding
config.json (True), covering the previously-missed branch.

Branch coverage: emitter.rs 92.79% → 93.27%; overall 92.22% → 92.27%.

Implement Pattern.COMMENTS flag and (?x) inline mode (#109)

Add support for Java's verbose/comments regex mode (Pattern.COMMENTS flag
and (?x)/(?-x) inline toggles):

- Add ParseFlags.COMMENTS flag (shifts NEVER_NL, NEVER_CAPTURE, WAS_DOLLAR,
  UNIX_LINES up by one bit position)
- Map Pattern.COMMENTS → ParseFlags.COMMENTS in toParseFlags()
- Skip unescaped whitespace and #-to-EOL comments in the main parse loop
  and inside character classes when COMMENTS is active
- Handle whitespace around '-' in character class range parsing
- Support (?x) enable, (?-x) disable, and (?x:...) scoped mode in
  parsePerlFlags()
- Add comprehensive CommentsTest covering: basic whitespace/comment
  stripping, character class behavior, inline flag toggling, flag
  combinations, and edge cases (empty patterns, \Q...\E quoting,
  LITERAL interaction)
